law: Plugin scanner must enumerate <plugin>/skills/SKILL.md and emit namespaced ids; MC-SA-02 fix
Audit unifier merger INPUTS before blaming merge logic on missing items
Pre-compact vault dump fires async on first CONTEXT WARNING via gsd-context-monitor.js spawn; /compact remains user-typed (UI command, hooks cannot dispatch slash commands)

Any /compact MUST be preceded by pre_compact_vault_dump.py — context purge below 65% used (35% remaining) without a sleepy-index pointer is forbidden. Snowball: future sessions cite this row instead of re-discussing context-management policy.

Workspace topology is immutable: any layout change must be backed by a Topology Engine artifact (vault/topology/lazarus_layout_*.json) AND a topology_apply.py --dry-run that resolves all macro slots to concrete paths. Live application is fenced behind --i-have-no-open-windows + 0 detected Cursor processes; otherwise refuse to clobber.

Topology layout MC-LAZ-41-MAP: 5/5 macro slots resolved (InfinityOps, KobiiCraft Workspace, Cursor Projects, LuckyFly, claude-power-pack) via explicit resolved_path field in lazarus_layout_2026-04-29.json. Cursor window labels can drift from on-disk folder names (KobiiCraft Core Files label vs KobiiCraft Workspace folder); layout JSON records both for forensic parity.

Topology label-vs-folder drift is canonical: workspace JSON records BOTH the Cursor window label (e.g. 'KobiiCraft Core Files') AND the on-disk resolved_path (e.g. 'Minecraft Projects/KobiiCraft Workspace'). Lazarus router consumes the resolved_path, not the label. Relative paths are forbidden in lazarus_layout_*.json.

Before triggering any destructive remediation script (OS reinstall, DISM repair, registry rewrite, force-push, schema migration) whose justification comes from a memory entry, RUN A LIVE PROBE FIRST against the alleged-broken system. Stale memory + destructive action = real damage risk. MC-SYS-09 misfire 2026-04-30: Docker was already working but I trusted a 2-day-old 'pending UAC click' memory and spawned REPAIR_WINDOWS_v2.bat. setup.exe failed-fast on 0xC1900204 (no data loss), but premise was wrong. Lesson: probe-before-repair, always.
